compiled_state.title: As a developer, implementation-plan presents multiple approaches before committing to one
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Modify /dso:implementation-plan to generate at least 3 structured implementation proposals before beginning task breakdown.
**Why**: Combats anchoring bias by forcing consideration of alternatives at the cheapest intervention point — before any code is written.
**Scope**:
- IN: Proposal generation logic, structured proposal format (title, description, files touched, pros, cons, risk profile), distinctness validation gate, integration into existing SKILL.md step flow
- OUT: Agent-based evaluation (Story 2), resolution loop (Story 3), eval suite (Story 4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, implementation-plan generates at least 3 structured proposals before task breakdown
  <- Satisfies: "implementation-plan generates at least 3 distinct implementation proposals for each story"
- When this story is complete, proposals are validated for structural distinctness (must differ on data layer, control flow, dependency, or interface boundary) before proceeding
  <- Satisfies: "implementation-plan generates at least 3 distinct implementation proposals"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Consumer Impact] implementation-plan is invoked by /dso:sprint via Skill tool. Sprint impl-plan-dispatch.md references Steps 1-6. Proposal generation must integrate without breaking STATUS output protocol or step numbering.
- [Design] When invoked from /dso:sprint, proposal selection must be non-interactive. Decision-maker handles selection autonomously in sprint context. When invoked standalone, user may see proposals. Define detection mechanism for sprint vs standalone context.
- [Reliability] Proposal distinctness validation needed — LLM may produce near-identical proposals
- [Maintainability] Proposal schema shared with agent — define single source of truth
